questions.question: Myocardial infarction (Heart Attack) occurs when:

options.A: Heart muscle is suddenly damaged by an inadequate blood supply
options.B: The heart fails to pump effectively over time
options.C: The heart completely stops beating out of nowhere

questions.question: Purkinje fibers are found extensively distributed in:

options.A: The ventricular musculature

questions.question: The difference between systolic and diastolic pressure is called:

options.A: Pulse pressure

questions.question: What triggers the conversion of prothrombin into thrombin during clotting?

options.A: Prothrombinase complex (Thrombokinase) and Ca++ ions

questions.question: The unique feature of mammalian circulation that completely separates oxygenated and deoxygenated blood is:

options.A: Double circulation facilitated by a 4-chambered heart

questions.question: In the context of ABO blood grouping, the antigens are structurally:

options.A: Oligosaccharides (glycoproteins/glycolipids) on the RBC membrane
options.B: Proteins freely floating in the plasma

questions.question: Which statement perfectly differentiates an artery from a vein?

options.A: Arteries have a thicker tunica media and lack valves compared to veins
options.C: Arteries always carry oxygenated blood (except pulmonary)
options.D: Veins carry blood away from the heart

questions.question: The primary pacemaker, the SA node, is located in the:

options.A: Right upper corner of the right atrium
options.B: Left upper corner of the left atrium

questions.question: Which cardiovascular structure acts as a temporary bypass to the lungs in the human fetus?

options.A: Ductus arteriosus / Foramen ovale

questions.question: A person with AB blood group needs a transfusion. What blood type can they safely receive?

options.A: Any blood type (A, B, AB, or O)

questions.question: Eosinophilia, an absolute increase in eosinophils, is generally a clinical indicator of:

options.A: Parasitic infections or severe allergic reactions

questions.question: Which blood parameter directly reflects the percentage of total blood volume occupied by erythrocytes?

options.A: Hematocrit (Packed Cell Volume)

questions.question: The duration of one complete cardiac cycle in a normal resting human is roughly:

options.A: 0.8 seconds

questions.question: Venous valves are most critical in the veins of the lower limbs to:

options.A: Prevent the backflow of blood under gravity

questions.question: Cardiac output is mathematically represented as:

options.A: Stroke Volume x Heart Rate
options.D: End Diastolic Volume - End Systolic Volume

questions.question: During isovolumetric contraction of the ventricles:

options.A: All heart valves are closed, and pressure rises rapidly
options.D: Blood is rapidly ejecting into the aorta

questions.question: Hypoxia in heart tissue primarily manifests as which clinical symptom?

options.A: Angina pectoris (chest pain)

questions.question: Which layer of the heart wall is responsible for its pumping action?

options.A: Myocardium

questions.question: To administer RhoGAM (anti-Rh antibodies) to prevent hemolytic disease of the newborn, the timing is typically:

options.A: Given to an Rh-negative mother shortly after delivering an Rh-positive baby

questions.question: Norepinephrine, secreted by the sympathetic nervous system, affects the heart by:

options.A: Increasing both heart rate and myocardial contractility
